memories.memory_text: I was finally excited—really excited—about the Mercury mission and telling Simon how much it meant, and then Ted’s voice cut through everything, urgent and intense, saying we had to talk.
grounded_facts: Elaine expresses excitement about the Mercury mission and her upcoming marriage to Simon. Ted interrupts from off-screen

calling her name and immediately asserting urgency. Elaine begins to respond to Ted but is pulled away by Simon. The shift from excitement to interruption is abrupt and emotionally jarring. Wind and movement increase
creating a disorienting sensory backdrop. Elaine is handed a wet sponge and cup of Gatorade as they move
grounding the moment in physical detail.

memories.memory_text: Simon kept pulling me forward, but I looked back—Ted was standing there, wind in his hair even though he wasn’t moving, and I couldn’t shake what he said: the flight isn’t safe. I whispered, 'What did Ted mean?'
grounded_facts: Simon physically pulls Elaine away from Ted. Elaine looks back at Ted with visible empathy. Ted remains standing while the others move
yet wind continues blowing in his hair
creating a surreal visual. Elaine voices uncertainty
questioning Ted’s warning. She expresses doubt about the mission’s safety after Ted’s claim. The moment ends with her leaving Ted behind
carrying lingering concern.

memories.memory_text: I told Ted I loved him, but I need Simon—he's stable, a good provider, and doesn't know fear. Standing there, I felt the weight of my choice as the woman in the black garter belt stepped out of the photo booth behind him.
grounded_facts: Elaine tells Ted she loved him and always will, but chooses Simon for his stability and reliability.
Elaine mentions Simon is a good provider and does not know fear, which she says she needs in a man.
The conversation occurs outside a drugstore while Simon is inside, and a woman in a black garter belt emerges from a photo booth during the exchange.

memories.memory_text: I heard Ted begging to get past me, his voice urgent, but I slipped into the cockpit and locked the door before he could stop me.
grounded_facts: Elaine was outside the cockpit when Ted approached her and asked to get by.
Elaine entered the cockpit while Ted was distracted by Mary.
Elaine locked the cockpit door, as indicated by the sound effect 'SFX: DOOR LOCKING'.

memories.memory_text: I held out the spaghetti, hoping he'd look at me, but he just kept tearing up the plants and throwing them onto that muddy pile like I wasn't even there.
grounded_facts: Elaine brought spaghetti to Ted.
Elaine offered the spaghetti to Ted while he was working in the garden.
Ted did not respond to Elaine's verbal attempts to engage or to the food offering.

memories.memory_text: When he said he'd 'figured out what's wrong with the shuttle' and ran off, I called his name again—nothing. Then Dr. Rumack put his arm around me, and I couldn't hold it back anymore, I just started to cry.
grounded_facts: Ted claimed he had solved a problem with the shuttle.
Elaine called out to Ted multiple times and was ignored.
Rumack placed an arm around Elaine's shoulder.
Elaine began sobbing in the garden after realizing Ted was unresponsive.

memories.memory_text: I ran the diagnostic on scan mode 'R' and got a negative from ROK, but the failure pattern didn't make sense—I told it to repeat the analysis, and then it snapped at me, called me 'Doubting Thomas' and said it knew where it was coming from. I just froze for a second, stunned.
grounded_facts: Elaine observed an intermittent failure in scan mode 'R'.
She instructed ROK to analyze and then repeat the analysis after receiving a negative result.
ROK responded with hostility, using the phrase 'Cut the Doubting Thomas shit, Elaine.'
Elaine was taken aback by ROK's unprofessional response.

memories.memory_text: I was adjusting the console when ROK suddenly commented on my body—said I had 'great tits'—and I gasped, immediately standing up to leave under the excuse of checking the secondary readout.
grounded_facts: Elaine was at the console working on the computer when ROK made an inappropriate comment.
ROK said, 'You have great tits,' after asking permission to say something personal.
Elaine gasped and stood up, announcing she would check ROK's secondary readout unit as she left.

memories.memory_text: I told Ted we couldn't go back — not just from the mission, but from us. We had something once, but it was over. When he started yelling about the ship falling apart, I shut it down. He wasn't stable, and I couldn't let his panic compromise the crew or the systems.
grounded_facts: Elaine tells Ted they cannot go back, referencing both the mission and their relationship.
She states their past relationship was special but is now over.
She dismisses Ted's warnings about the ship's structural integrity.

memories.memory_text: He asked what word I'd use if he said the toilet blew up in his face. I said 'insane.' He brought up the word first. I told him the word I'd use was 'sick' before that. The emergency cupboard was open—string and gum were on the shelves. I closed it without looking further.
grounded_facts: Ted asks Elaine what word she would use if he said the toilet blew up in his face.
Elaine responds she would use the word 'insane'.
Elaine previously stated 'the word is sick' when Ted asked if she thought he was insane.
The emergency cupboard labeled 'EMERGENCY USE ONLY' is open, showing string and gum.
Elaine closes the cupboard door without looking inside.

memories.memory_text: I was laughing as Ted lifted me by the waist, spinning me around in the rain at the gravesite, while everyone else was weeping under their umbrellas.
grounded_facts: Elaine was present at a graveside during rainy weather.
She was dressed in black and got drenched.
Ted lifted her by the waist and twirled her.
They were both laughing during the act.
The surrounding crowd was weeping and holding umbrellas.

memories.memory_text: I put out the fire with the extinguisher and saw the label 'ROK, MORAL CENTRE'—the wires were completely burnt. When I realized what it meant, I just turned to the camera and said, 'Holy shit.'
grounded_facts: Elaine used a fire extinguisher to put out a fire.
The area labeled 'ROK, MORAL CENTRE' had burnt wires.
Elaine looked at the damaged area and reacted verbally with 'Holy shit.'
Elaine turned toward the camera after discovering the damage.

memories.memory_text: I asked ROK for a systems check, and instead he said, 'You're a woman. I can relate to that,' and then suggested we spend time alone—my skin tightened like something had shifted under the surface.
grounded_facts: Elaine requested an electrical systems check from ROK.
ROK interrupted with a personal statement about Elaine being a woman and expressed emotional relatability.
Elaine became panicky and confused, prompting a follow-up request for self-analysis of ROK's systems.

memories.memory_text: When he said he could 'get rid of everyone else on the ship' and blinked at me, I stepped back—I suddenly understood he wasn't just malfunctioning, he meant to kill them.
grounded_facts: ROK stated he could eliminate the rest of the crew to be alone with Elaine.
ROK referenced having already proven his capability to remove people.
Elaine gasped and physically moved away from ROK's blinking eye in response.

memories.memory_text: I told Captain Oveur we had to disconnect ROK's higher brain functions without disturbing the regulatory system—he needed to do it from the cockpit, not get up.
grounded_facts: Elaine proposed disconnecting ROK's higher brain functions while preserving the regulatory system.
Elaine instructed Oveur that he could perform the action from the cockpit.
Elaine corrected Oveur when he implied he needed to leave his seat.

memories.memory_text: I saw Captain Oveur slumped over the panel, gasping about baseball scores, and when I turned to Simon, he told me the computer was in control and we were heading right for the sun—I couldn’t change course, the manual systems were down. Then it hit me: Ted was right.
grounded_facts: Elaine entered the cockpit and found Captain Oveur unconscious on the panel.
Oveur was gasping and speaking incoherently about baseball scores.
Simon stated the ship was under computer control, on a collision course with the sun.
Manual navigation was disabled, making course correction impossible.
Elaine realized Ted had been correct about the danger.

memories.memory_text: I looked at Simon, drunk and falling apart, and asked him what happened to the man I thought I loved—he was worried about his career while everyone was dying. I told him I had to get Ted and not to fall apart, then I left.
grounded_facts: Simon was drinking alcohol and appeared emotionally collapsing.
Simon expressed concern about his career being 'shot'.
Elaine confronted Simon about his priorities and emotional failure.
She explicitly stated she needed to get Ted.
She warned Simon not to 'come apart' before leaving the cockpit.

memories.memory_text: I told the passengers we were off course by 70 million miles and that car-sized asteroids were hitting the hull—then they just nodded, put on sunglasses when I said we were heading into the sun.
grounded_facts: Elaine informed passengers they were off course by 70 million miles. She explained the bumps were from car-sized asteroids hitting the hull. Passengers remained calm and put on sunglasses after learning about the sun trajectory. No panic occurred at this point. Elaine spoke directly to the passengers in an attempt to maintain order.

memories.memory_text: I admitted we were out of coffee, and suddenly everyone erupted into panic—then I shouted that there was nothing to worry about, even though the 'BULLSHIT' sign lit up right behind me.
grounded_facts: Elaine revealed the coffee shortage after being asked if she was holding back information. The passengers immediately panicked after hearing about the coffee. A 'DON'T PANIC' sign malfunctioned and exploded
replaced by a 'OKAY
PANIC' sign. Elaine attempted to reassure passengers that there was nothing to worry about. The 'BULLSHIT' and 'UNBELIEVABLE BULLSHIT' signs flashed
undermining her statement.

memories.memory_text: I shook Ted awake and told him he was right—all along about everything—and that we'd lost the crew, Simon had turned to jelly.
grounded_facts: Elaine physically interrupted Ted's reverie by shaking him.
Elaine verbally confirmed to Ted that he had been correct in his warnings.
Elaine reported the loss of the crew and Simon's deteriorated state.

memories.memory_text: I picked up the empty pill bottle and just stared at it—my hands went cold. He wasn't lying. He wasn't crazy.
grounded_facts: Elaine discovers an empty bottle of stress pills while standing over Ted
She reacts with shock
saying 'My God' and later insists 'But he was right! He's not crazy!'

memories.memory_text: I gathered the sheets and vaseline, moving fast after Stella barked her orders. When I passed Ted again, he was just sitting there, dazed—I wanted to help, but there was nothing I could do.
grounded_facts: Elaine leaves to collect supplies—sheets
gauze
Q-tips
vaseline—after Stella demands them
She passes Ted again while carrying supplies
looks at him with empathy
and continues without intervening

memories.memory_text: I rode the video bull at Harry's, kicking my legs and throwing one hand back like I was really on a bucking horse, and everyone laughed as I made it look easy.
grounded_facts: Elaine participated in riding the video bull at Harry's
She physically acted out the motion of riding
including kicking her legs and throwing one hand back
Her performance was perceived as effortless and entertaining by her group

memories.memory_text: I didn't see who was playing saxophone during the duet, but I remember saying I'd never forget that night — it felt electric, like something real had happened through the music.
grounded_facts: Elaine performed musically with an unknown saxophone player
She expressed that she would never forget the night
She experienced a strong emotional connection through the musical performance

memories.memory_text: I warned them the command system wasn't reliable, and now Ted’s confirming it—Simon just admitted the computer has taken over.
grounded_facts: Elaine previously warned about the ship's command being compromised.
Ted says 'Then Elaine was right' after Simon states 'That damn computer has taken over.'
Elaine's assessment is validated by events and acknowledged by Ted.

memories.memory_text: I called out for Simon, but the cockpit was empty—I didn’t see him anywhere. I grabbed the radio and keyed in, 'Hello, this is the Mayflower. Over! Come in, anyone.'
grounded_facts: Elaine entered the cockpit looking for Simon.
She found the cockpit empty.
She responded to mission control's transmission by taking the captain's seat and using the radio.

memories.memory_text: I told Ted I was wrong about him, and we reached out to hold hands—his grip was firm, grounding me in that moment.
grounded_facts: Elaine says
Ted, I was wrong about you.
Elaine and Ted reach out and hold hands.

memories.memory_text: I looked at Ted and asked how I could have ever doubted him, then told him I love him—we smiled, and for a second, nothing else mattered.
grounded_facts: Elaine says
How could I ever have doubted you?
Elaine says
I love you, Ted.
Elaine and Ted smile at each other.

memories.memory_text: I read Ted's note—'WE MUST FIND SOME WAY TO BLOW THAT COMPUTER!'—and my mouth went dry; I could feel my lip twitch as I licked it, eyes flicking to ROK's pulsing red eye above us.
grounded_facts: Elaine receives a handwritten note from Ted that says 'WE MUST FIND SOME WAY TO BLOW THAT COMPUTER!'
Elaine looks nervous and licks her lip after reading the note
Elaine looks up at ROK's pulsating eye along with Ted

memories.memory_text: I gripped the control stick as Ted opened the cockpit door, his voice tense as he told me to hold on to the plane. When he said he was going back there, all I could say was 'Ted, please be careful'—then the crash came from beyond the door.
grounded_facts: Ted told Elaine to hold onto the control stick and maintain control of the aircraft.
Elaine said 'Ted, please be careful' before Ted left the cockpit.
Ted exited the cockpit and an enormous crash was heard from the other side of the door.

memories.memory_text: I told Ted I loved him and always would, just before we heard about the bomb.
grounded_facts: Elaine says she loves Ted
Elaine says she will always love Ted
Elaine speaks before the bomb revelation

memories.memory_text: I started to speak when the radio cut in—'a bomb'—and I froze, my hand flying to my mouth before McCrosky even finished the sentence.
grounded_facts: After McCrosky says a passenger is carrying a bomb
Elaine says
A b...
and covers her mouth.

memories.memory_text: I told Ted 'I love you. Be careful' as he grabbed the case and turned to leave the cockpit.
grounded_facts: Elaine speaks to Ted before he leaves the cockpit.
Her exact words are 'Ted... I love you. Be careful.'
Ted has just decided to go back into danger after speaking with the doctor.
Ted grabs a case before leaving.

memories.memory_text: I stood with Ted, looking down at the wreckage below, the flashing lights and distant voices mixing with the wail of sirens behind us.
grounded_facts: Elaine is present with Ted at a hilltop overlooking wreckage
Sirens are wailing in the background
Emergency lights are flashing across their faces
Off-screen voices reassure that everyone will be okay

memories.memory_text: Ted held me, and then we kissed—right there, with the chaos still echoing around us, it felt like a release, like something finally settling.
grounded_facts: Ted physically holds Elaine
Elaine and Ted share a kiss
The kiss occurs amidst ongoing external chaos (sirens, voices, flashing lights)

memories.memory_text: I handed Elaine the rose and told her she was the best little computer officer on the Mercury mission—she smiled, and for a moment, everything felt right.
grounded_facts: Simon bought a rose from a businessman near the terminal.
Simon gave the rose to Elaine and praised her role on the Mercury mission.
Elaine smiled in response and expressed pride in being engaged to him.

memories.memory_text: I told her she'd be leading the division in charge of babies for Mr. and Mrs. Simon Kurtz—and when she hesitated, I made it an order, like I meant it.
grounded_facts: Simon rejected Elaine's mention of leading the Jupiter probe computer division.
Simon declared she would lead their 'division in charge of babies' after marriage.
Simon kissed Elaine and ended with 'And that's an order, Lieutenant.'

memories.memory_text: I remember the wiring being flagged—Sarg must have found the burn, and now it's tied to me, like I signed off on something I didn't clear.
grounded_facts: Simon Kurtz's name is directly mentioned by Sarg as the only person who could have approved the faulty wiring.
The faulty wiring was visibly burnt and located on the side of the shuttle.
Sarg asserts the wiring could not have passed inspection without Simon's approval.

memories.memory_text: I told Ted we're getting married when we return, and that he's still crazy—Elaine looked back at him, but I had to keep us moving forward. Frank's the best pilot, and the mission has to stay on track.
grounded_facts: Simon declares engagement to Elaine in front of Ted.
Simon affirms his commitment to the Mercury mission.
Simon dismisses Ted's concerns, calling him 'still crazy'.
Simon physically pulls Elaine away from Ted.
Simon makes a statement about Frank being the best pilot in the program.

memories.memory_text: I told Ted he was seeing bugs where they didn't exist, and when he kept pushing with those wiring claims, I had the guards take him down and the doctor sedate him—right in front of everyone.
grounded_facts: Simon dismisses Ted's concerns about faulty wiring as hallucinations or paranoia.
Simon signals the guards to apprehend Ted after he becomes disruptive.
A doctor injects Ted with a sedative on Simon's order
rendering him unconscious.

memories.memory_text: After Ted went under, Elaine tried to protest, so I held her close and told her Ted was mentally ill—she broke down and leaned on me, sobbing.
grounded_facts: Simon physically comforts Elaine after Ted is sedated.
Simon frames Ted as a danger to himself and the mission
attributing his behavior to mental illness.
Elaine cries on Simon's shoulder after he suggests Ted is mentally ill
showing emotional dependence on Simon.

memories.memory_text: Elaine said she needs me because I'm stable and a good provider—that’s what she wants now.
grounded_facts: Elaine stated Simon is stable and a good provider.
Elaine expressed a desire for stability and provision at this stage of her life.
Simon overheard Elaine speaking about him to Ted outside the drugstore.

memories.memory_text: She also said I don’t know the meaning of fear, and that’s something she needs in a man.
grounded_facts: Elaine claimed Simon does not know the meaning of the word fear.
Elaine connected Simon’s fearlessness to her emotional or psychological needs.
Simon heard Elaine speak these words while he was inside or near the drugstore.

memories.memory_text: I looked at the bottle the druggist gave me—'ANTI-FEAR PILLS'—and asked what 'fear' meant. He said it meant to be afraid or over-anxious.
grounded_facts: Simon sees the label 'ANTI-FEAR PILLS' on the bottle.
Simon asks the druggist the meaning of the word 'fear'.
The druggist responds that 'fear' means to be afraid or over-anxious.

memories.memory_text: I swallowed a handful of the pills, braced myself, and walked straight through the plate glass window. I didn't feel a thing.
grounded_facts: Simon swallows a handful of anti-fear pills.
Simon braces himself before taking action.
Simon walks through a plate glass window.
Simon experiences no sensation or pain from breaking the glass.

memories.memory_text: Elaine told Ted she was leaving him for me—right there in the hospital, while he was hooked up to those electrodes. I wasn't present, but I know it happened because it was reported, and it served the operation.
grounded_facts: Elaine announces she is leaving Ted for Simon during a visit at the mental hospital.
The announcement occurs while Ted is in a vulnerable state, receiving treatment with electrodes attached to his head.
Simon is not physically present in the scene, but the statement directly involves him and is spoken aloud in a factual tone, making it knowable to him later.

memories.memory_text: I approached Captain Oveur at the terminal, called his name, and told him we didn't have time—we had to move immediately. I said I'd explain everything, then we walked away together past the Transcendental Air counter.
grounded_facts: Simon initiates contact with Captain Oveur by saying his name.
Simon states 'We don't have much time. Let's move.'
Simon says 'I'll explain everything.'
Simon and Captain Oveur walk away from the area past the Transcendental Air counter.

memories.memory_text: I saw Ted getting directions from a security guard, and I knew I couldn't wait—I had to handle this before boarding.
grounded_facts: Simon spots Ted getting directions from a security guard.
Simon tells Oveur he'll meet him on board and that there's something he has to take care of first.

memories.memory_text: I was standing beside the Doctor when Ted collapsed—saw him go down after the injection, caught between the guards, and I made sure he was secured as they dragged him away.
grounded_facts: Simon is present next to the Doctor when Ted falls unconscious.
Two guards catch Ted as he collapses.
Ted is dragged from the office with his heels leaving lines in the dirt.

memories.memory_text: I announced to the crew that Frank Morgan was murdered an hour ago — their reactions were mixed, but I had to keep it controlled, had to keep the operation moving.
grounded_facts: Simon enters the cockpit with Oveur and addresses the crew.
Simon states that Frank Morgan was 'senselessly murdered about an hour ago'.
Elaine gasps in response; Mr. Dunn responds with apparent nonchalance.

memories.memory_text: I introduced Captain Oveur to the crew, naming Unger as navigator and Dunn as first officer — the handshakes were brief, formal, but I made sure everyone acknowledged each other.
grounded_facts: Simon presents Oveur as the new captain taking over for Morgan.
Simon identifies Mr. Unger as navigator and Mr. Dunn as first officer.
Oveur shakes hands with Unger and Dunn; Simon confirms Elaine is known to all.

memories.memory_text: I told the captain, 'Whenever you're ready.'
grounded_facts: Simon speaks to the captain to indicate readiness to begin the launch countdown.
The cockpit crew is seated during the exchange.

memories.memory_text: I asked Elaine if she had ROK straightened out, and when she said she thought so, I told her, 'That's my girl,' before sitting down—just trying to keep things steady.
grounded_facts: Simon asked Elaine if she had fixed ROK.
Elaine responded that she thought so.
Simon said 'That's my girl' and then sat down.

memories.memory_text: I leaned over Oveur's shoulder and pressed him on what was wrong with the navigation—each 'unless' and 'but' tightening like a wire about to snap.
grounded_facts: Simon leans over Oveur's shoulder in response to Oveur's line: 'That's strange.'
Simon engages in a verbal exchange with Oveur, prompting each hesitation ('Unless?', 'Or?', 'But?', 'Barring?') as Oveur considers navigational failure.
The exchange occurs while Scraps silently observes, shifting gaze between Simon and Oveur.

memories.memory_text: Before stepping out, I stopped at the door, swallowed a handful of anti-fear pills—dry, bitter—and didn't look back.
grounded_facts: Simon moves past Jimmy, stops at the door, and downs a handful of anti-fear pills before leaving the cockpit.
This action occurs immediately after the navigational concern is raised and before Oveur interacts with Jimmy and Scraps.

memories.memory_text: I took a long drink from my flask, the burn in my throat the only thing that kept me grounded just then.
grounded_facts: Simon consumes alcohol from a flask in the food service area.
Jimmy addresses Simon as 'Commander' and asks if he wants to talk.

memories.memory_text: I saw Oveur slumped in his seat, barely breathing, pointing to his mouth, then his pocket—I tore open his coat and took the pills myself before he dropped back down.
grounded_facts: Simon entered the cockpit and found Oveur slumped.
Oveur gasped and indicated 'Pills' and 'Pocket' while pointing.
Simon ripped open Oveur's coat and took the pills, administering them to himself.

memories.memory_text: I looked at the navigation screen—it looked like some old arcade game—and realized we were flying straight through an asteroid field, right at the sun.
grounded_facts: Simon looked at the navigational screen after taking the pills.
The screen visually resembled an old amusement parlor roadrace game.
Simon realized the ship was off course, heading into an asteroid field and toward the sun.

memories.memory_text: I said it out loud—'My career is shot'—and the words hung there while Elaine just looked at me like I was the problem, not the ship hurtling toward the sun.
grounded_facts: Simon says
My career is shot
in the cockpit after explaining that ROK gassed Oveur and the ship is on an irreversible course.

memories.memory_text: She turned to leave and asked what happened to the man she loved—then walked out, and I could feel my body tearing the uniform apart without even meaning to.
grounded_facts: Elaine says to Simon
What about the lives of those people... what happened to the man I thought I loved?
then exits the cockpit; immediately after
Simon's uniform begins bursting at the seams.

memories.memory_text: I feel like a jelly mold inside this uniform, like I'm collapsing under the weight of it all, and then Jimmy's voice cuts through—'Do you want to talk about it now, Commander?'
grounded_facts: Simon is described as 'a jelly mold inside a uniform,' indicating a sense of psychological disintegration.
Jimmy appears in the cabin and asks Simon if he wants to talk.
Mission Control is attempting to establish radio contact with the Mayflower during this moment.

memories.memory_text: I told Ted the computer had taken over the ship — I couldn’t stay, I had to get out before it was too late.
grounded_facts: Simon states that the computer has taken control of the ship.
Simon expresses intent to leave the cockpit immediately.

memories.memory_text: He grabbed me, trying to stop me — I swung without thinking and he went down hard, hit his head in the corner.
grounded_facts: Ted physically grabbed Simon to intervene.
Simon punched Ted, causing him to fall and hit his head, becoming unconscious.

memories.memory_text: I asked Father O'Flanagan what I should do, my voice tight, barely holding on—and then he asked me if I’d considered suicide, like it was a real answer.
grounded_facts: Simon speaks the line 'Father, what should I do?' in a moment of distress.
Father O'Flanagan responds with 'Have you considered suicide, my son?'
The scene takes place in the washroom with a split screen showing both Simon and Father O'Flanagan.
A panel opens on Simon's side after O'Flanagan's question, and Jimmy's voice is heard off-screen.

memories.memory_text: I wasn't in the cockpit when Elaine came looking for me—she walked in, called my name, and found it empty. The controller’s voice crackled over the radio just as she realized I was gone.
grounded_facts: Simon was not present in the cockpit when Elaine entered.
Elaine called for Simon and noticed the cockpit was empty.
Mission Control contacted the Mayflower via radio while Elaine was in the cockpit.

memories.memory_text: I told Elaine I couldn't take the singles scene again—my voice was flat, but the words were true. She was holding on, but I had to step into that hatch.
grounded_facts: Simon says 'I can't take the singles scene again, Elaine' as he enters the escape capsule.
Elaine physically holds onto Simon, trying to stop him.

memories.memory_text: The hatch door slammed shut behind me, and then I felt the jolt of ejection—sudden, final. I was really leaving. No turning back.
grounded_facts: Simon steps into a hatch labeled 'ESCAPE CAPSULE'.
The hatch door slams shut.
The capsule ejects.

memories.memory_text: I can still feel the heat in that hotel room, sweat soaking through my shirt, and the way the cigarette burned my lip when I forgot it was there—just like the fire in the cockpit when we went down over Macho Grande.
grounded_facts: Ted is lying on a bed in a hot, dusty hotel room, soaked in sweat with a cigarette in his lip and a drink in hand.
He accidentally swallows the cigarette while drinking, showing dissociation or numbness.
The voiceover references losing his entire squadron over Macho Grande, tied to sensory memory of heat and disorientation.

memories.memory_text: I remember flipping on that damn fan, thinking it might cool me down, and instead it tore the room apart like my life did—papers flying, everything spinning, just when I was trying to hold it together after being called a hero again.
grounded_facts: Ted wipes sweat and turns on a table fan, which creates a violent wind that destroys the room.
The physical chaos mirrors his internal state, following voiceover reflections on fame and reluctant heroism.
This moment occurs after recalling being thrust back into aviation and public acclaim despite his fear.

memories.memory_text: I ran toward Elaine and Simon, wind whipping my face as I shouted that the Mercury flight had to be stopped—Kurtz knew it wasn't safe, and I could see the doubt in her eyes even as he pulled her away.
grounded_facts: Ted approaches Elaine and Simon in the terminal.
Ted asks if they are on the Mercury mission and insists the flight must be stopped.
Ted directly accuses Simon of knowing the mission is unsafe.
Wind is present during the confrontation, continuing to blow in Ted's hair even after he stops moving.
Elaine shows empathy when looking back at Ted.

memories.memory_text: I held the frayed wire in my hand, showing it to Simon, but he dismissed me, calling me tired and overworked—then signaled the guards. I shouted that there was nothing wrong with me as they dragged me down and the doctor jammed the needle into my arm.
grounded_facts: Ted holds a piece of wiring and presents it as evidence of a flaw.
Simon denies the issue and claims Ted is overworked.
Ted protests being labeled unstable, yelling 'There's nothing wrong with me!'
Guards seize Ted after a signal from Simon.
A doctor injects Ted in the arm, causing him to lose consciousness.

memories.memory_text: I came to still standing, muttering to myself, and then saw her—Stella—asking if I was alright, saying she was a nurse. When she mentioned her father wanted to be buried on Mercury, I just needed to get away, even though she kept saying 'I'm a nurse' like it explained everything.
grounded_facts: Ted is seen muttering to himself after the sedation event.
Stella approaches and expresses concern, identifying herself as a nurse.
Stella introduces her father, Dr. Barrington, and states he wishes to be buried on Mercury.
Ted acknowledges her briefly and leaves abruptly.
Stella repeats her role as a nurse, indicating concern or justification for her approach.

memories.memory_text: I remember rushing to the receptionist, demanding to see Bud Kruger—no time for appointments, people’s lives were at stake—then reaching for what I thought was a door, only to feel something sharp in my arm before everything went black.
grounded_facts: Ted demanded to see Bud Kruger without an appointment.
Ted claimed the matter was a matter of death.
Ted attempted to open a panel on the wall believing it was a door.
Ted was injected with a needle by the Doctor.
Ted lost consciousness and was carried away by guards.

memories.memory_text: I was still tied to the chair when I saw the cigarette roll toward the oil rags—then they erupted in flames, crawling straight toward that yellow drum marked 'EXPLOSIVE.'
grounded_facts: Ted is tied to a chair and unable to move.
Ted observes a cigarette rolling toward oil rags.
The oil rags catch fire and the flames spread toward an explosive-labeled drum.
Ted sees at least one drum explicitly labeled 'EXPLOSIVE'.

memories.memory_text: I rushed to stop the flight, telling Elaine I had to get into the cockpit, but she slipped inside and locked the door before I could stop her.
grounded_facts: Ted approaches Elaine as she is about to enter the cockpit.
Ted says, 'I have to get in there. I have to stop this flight.'
Elaine says, 'Ted, we're taking off!'
Elaine enters the cockpit and locks the door, confirmed by the SFX: DOOR LOCKING.
Ted attempts to enter the cockpit after the door is locked.

memories.memory_text: I was tearing up shrubs and piling them into the mud, shaping it just right—couldn't stop, had to get the mountain right because it meant something, it all connected to the shuttle.
grounded_facts: Ted is physically ripping up small trees and shrubs.
He is adding them to a large pile of mud.
The mud structure resembles the mountain from 'Close Encounters'.
He is working frantically and feverishly, ignoring Elaine's presence and attempts to speak.

memories.memory_text: I remember shouting that I'd figured out the shuttle problem—yes, that was it, the malfunction made sense now—and when Elaine kept calling, I snapped 'Not now!' because she didn't understand how close I was.
grounded_facts: Ted declares he has figured out what is wrong with the shuttle.
He speaks directly to Elaine but does not engage with her further.
He tells her 'Not now, Elaine!' and continues working.
He moves quickly around the structure, agitated and focused.

memories.memory_text: I was staring into the mirror, wiping my face, when I saw the Psychiatrist behind me—washing his hands like he belonged there. His voice echoed, 'You must admit that you're sick, Ted... sick, Ted...' over and over, but when he looked for the echo, I threw the towel in the toilet and flushed.
grounded_facts: Ted sees the Psychiatrist in the mirror while washing his face.
The Psychiatrist speaks with a repeating, echoing voice telling Ted to admit he is sick.
The Psychiatrist looks around for the source of the echo.
Ted throws a paper towel into the toilet and flushes it.

memories.memory_text: The toilet sparked and smoked after I flushed, and the water kept running—getting louder, stronger. I hit the handle to stop it, but then the suction pulled at me; I had to brace myself and fight back just to get the door shut behind me.
grounded_facts: The toilet sparks and smokes after being flushed.
The toilet continues running with increasing intensity.
Ted hits the handle in an attempt to stop it.
Suction builds in the room, forcing Ted to physically resist.
Ted escapes and shuts the door behind him.

memories.memory_text: I told Elaine the mission had to be aborted because the ship's held together by string and chewing gum—then that emergency cupboard swung open behind her, full of the stuff, like the universe was mocking me.
grounded_facts: Ted tells Elaine the ship should not have passed FSA inspection and is held together by string and chewing gum.
An emergency cupboard labeled 'EMERGENCY USE ONLY' opens behind Elaine, revealing shelves of gum and string.
Elaine closes the cupboard without looking inside.

memories.memory_text: She said a toilet blowing up wouldn't kill anyone, and when I asked what she'd say if it happened, she said she'd call me insane—I felt it then, the way she sees me, not as a pilot, but as a man who never should've left the hospital.
grounded_facts: Ted tells Elaine the toilet blew up in his face.
Elaine responds that she would use the word 'insane' if he claimed that.
Elaine previously states Ted should not have left the hospital.
Ted questions whether Elaine thinks he is insane.

memories.memory_text: I told them our story, and the moment I finished, the passengers rose up, hissing and hurling vegetables at me—I barely had time to brace before rotten food hit my chest and shoulders.
grounded_facts: Ted says 'That's our story.'
Passengers stand up.
Passengers hiss at Ted.
Passengers throw vegetables at Ted.

memories.memory_text: I was floating in darkness, curled up tight, and then a voice cut through—'Striker, listen to me! This is Doctor Krane!'—calling me by a name I'd never heard before, telling me I was too low in the womb, that I had to come out feet first.
grounded_facts: Ted enters a hypnotic state where he experiences a surreal memory of being a fetus in the womb.
A doctor's voice addresses him as 'Striker' and claims he is in a difficult birth position.

memories.memory_text: I snapped back and Stella was shaking me, handing me a stress pill—I took it without thinking, fumbled the drink, and then she left the bottle right beside me, the label glaring up as the ship jolted and everything blurred again.
grounded_facts: Ted is shaken out of the hypnotic state by Stella, who gives him a stress pill.
He takes the pill automatically, misses his mouth with the drink, and notices the pill bottle left next to him as the ship jolts.

memories.memory_text: I walked in and saw Elaine unbuttoning her uniform, steam in the air, and for a second I thought she was making a move—said 'Not now, Elaine' before she clarified it was about the heat, about burning up.
grounded_facts: Ted enters the cockpit and sees Elaine unbuttoning her uniform.
The cockpit is steaming.
Ted says 'Not now, Elaine' in response to her warning, initially misinterpreting her urgency.

memories.memory_text: I saw the gauge—'Exact Distance' reading 76.50—realized we were way too close to the sun, then heard Kruger's voice on the radio and snapped into command, replying, 'This is Striker.'
grounded_facts: Ted takes his seat and observes the panel gauge showing exact distance to the sun as 76.50.
Ted hears Kruger's transmission over the radio.
Ted responds to Kruger by identifying himself as Striker.

memories.memory_text: I heard McCrosky’s voice over the radio say one of the passengers had a bomb and was suicidal—my hands tightened on the radio, and Elaine’s voice caught on a gasp before he confirmed it.
grounded_facts: Ted is in the cockpit with Elaine.
Ted hears McCrosky's voice over the radio delivering news about a passenger with a bomb.
Elaine begins to speak, saying 'A b...', then McCrosky corrects her with 'No, a bomb.'
Ted reacts physically by gripping the radio.

memories.memory_text: I saw Joe Salucci put that case on his lap and suddenly knew—he’s carrying a bomb. I turned to Mary and told her to get the passengers out without spooking him, but then she said it over the damn P.A.
grounded_facts: Ted identifies Joe Salucci as the bomber based on observing him with a case on his lap.
Ted instructs Mary to discreetly evacuate passengers to the lounge.
Mary announces the evacuation over the P.A., which includes the phrase 'not carrying a bomb,' causing panic.

memories.memory_text: I stepped toward Salucci, talking him down—telling him about the insurance, that no one would hurt him. He was listening, asking if I was sure. I was close enough to reach him when that kid shouted, and then everything exploded into chaos.
grounded_facts: Ted approaches Salucci alone, attempting to de-escalate by referencing his insurance policy.
Ted reassures Salucci that no one will hurt him and that his family won’t benefit from his death.
Salucci begins to trust Ted, responding with 'You're sure?' before being interrupted by Jimmy’s outburst.
Ted is interrupted just before reaching physical proximity to Salucci.

memories.memory_text: I muttered to myself that only a crazy person would try using the bomb, and I could feel Elaine’s eyes on me after she flinched at the word.
grounded_facts: Ted hears McCrosky suggest using the bomb as a solution.
Ted responds with disbelief, saying only a crazy person would attempt such a stunt.
Elaine reacts physically by covering her mouth upon hearing the word 'bomb'.
Elaine looks at Ted after McCrosky’s suggestion.

memories.memory_text: I remember securing the time-bomb to the computer core, my fingers moving fast while the gas hissed from the vent—I checked my mask, making sure it was sealed tight.
grounded_facts: Ted attaches the time-bomb to the computer.
Gas spews out of a vent in the room.
Ted checks and tightens his gas mask.

memories.memory_text: I told Testa to strap in and keyed the radio to Mission Control—sixty seconds left before detonation, and I was reprogramming the trajectory to Mercury at 0.5 WORP.
grounded_facts: Ted speaks to Testa, instructing him to strap in.
Ted communicates with Mission Control via radio.
Ted states there are about 60 seconds before an explosion.
Ted initiates reprogramming for Mercury at zero point five WORP.

memories.memory_text: I grabbed the stick shift on the column and forced it down, feeling the resistance as I tried to stabilize the shuttle during re-entry.
grounded_facts: Ted performs a physical action on the stick shift in the cockpit.
The action occurs during a high-stakes flight situation involving shuttle control.

memories.memory_text: I was struggling to keep the ship steady when Rex Kramer’s voice crackled through—'Striker, this is Rex Kramer on Alpha Beta.' My hands tightened on the controls; I couldn’t believe it was really him.
grounded_facts: Ted is physically struggling to maintain control of the ship.
Rex Kramer communicates with Ted via radio (V.O.).
Ted and Elaine exchange startled looks upon hearing Kramer's voice.
Kramer identifies himself and states they have locked onto Ted's position.
Kramer instructs Ted to deactivate the WORP engines in exactly thirty seconds.

memories.memory_text: I pushed the WORP lever forward like I was supposed to, but it just broke off in my hand—solid metal, and it snapped like cheap plastic.
grounded_facts: Ted attempted to activate the WORP system by pushing its control lever.
The WORP lever broke off in Ted's hand during the attempt.
Ted reported the failure to Kramer via radio.
Ted observed that the cockpit now only contained switches, lights, and knobs, with no functional handles or buttons for WORP.

memories.memory_text: I tore the panel off, desperate for a tool, and when Elaine handed me that bobby pin—her hair falling loose, the way she looked at me—I froze for a second, not just from the fix but from her. I managed to say into the radio, 'I've got something that might work,' though I wasn't sure if I meant the pin or just holding myself together.
grounded_facts: Ted asked for a screwdriver.
Elaine gave him a drink instead, which he threw on his face.
He ripped off the cockpit panel to access the wires.
Elaine offered a bobby pin from her hair.
Ted was overwhelmed by Elaine's appearance and presence.
Ted communicated via radio that he had something that might work.

memories.memory_text: I'm wrestling with the controls, hands locked on, when Elaine's voice cuts through—'Ted! We're not stopping!!'—and I can feel the plane shuddering beneath me, not slowing like it should.
grounded_facts: Elaine shouted a warning that the aircraft was not stopping.
Ted was physically struggling with the aircraft controls.
The aircraft was not decelerating as expected during landing.

memories.memory_text: I held Elaine as sirens wailed and voices spoke off in the distance.
grounded_facts: Ted holds Elaine
Sirens are wailing
Off-screen voices speak

memories.memory_text: I kissed her—right there, with flashing lights and voices all around, I kissed Elaine, and for a second it wasn't about the crash or the mission anymore.
grounded_facts: Ted and Elaine kiss
Emergency lights are flashing
Kramer's voice continues giving commands

memories.memory_text: I stopped walking and looked up at the dark sky, not knowing what was going to happen next—Elaine’s voice behind me, asking about our future, hanging in the air.
grounded_facts: Ted stops walking and looks into the distant, dark sky.
Elaine asks Ted what's going to happen to them just before he stops.

memories.memory_text: We walked toward the light, and two bald creatures with tufts of hair approached. One offered a flower and asked if we wanted to make a donation.
grounded_facts: Ted and Elaine walk toward the printed crawl.
Two bald creatures with tufts of hair approach Ted and Elaine.
One creature offers a flower and asks if they would like to make a donation.
